summary: Iter-3 testing of VIQSO Voter CRM: candidate profile, voter slip, image upload, AAP sample org. Backend: 69/69 pytest pass (56 iter-1/2 regression + 13 new iter-3). Frontend: AAP login (3-factor), Voters list (45 rows for AAP), Voter Slip /slip/{id} (party logo + candidate photo + bio + voter/booth details + Hindi footer + Print + WhatsApp buttons), Admin → Branding → Candidate Profile section, all verified end-to-end. Both iter-2 frontend blockers (access_key field, super-admin org list) are FIXED — access_key input is now visible on /login with data-testid='login-access-key-input'. No critical bugs found in iter-3.

minor.issue: [CARRY-OVER from iter-2] Line ~846 `db.booths.find({})` still loads booths without org_id filter. If two orgs share a booth_number, an admin's import could resolve to another org's booth_id. Filter by org_id.

[CARRY-OVER from iter-2] Brute-force lockout keyed by `{request.client.host}:{email}`; behind ingress the source IP varies, weakening the 5-fail lockout. Recommend keying by email only and/or X-Forwarded-For.
[CARRY-OVER] Returns dict `{families:[...],total_families:n}` while sibling list endpoints return plain arrays — inconsistent.

action_items: (Nice-to-have) Add org_id filter in /api/import/voters booth lookup to prevent cross-tenant booth_number collisions.
(Nice-to-have) Strengthen brute-force lockout: key by email only, honor X-Forwarded-For.
(Nice-to-have) Normalize /api/families response shape to plain array.
(Code quality) server.py is 1633 lines — split into routers (auth/users/booths/voters/visits/analytics/import/segregation/settings/orgs/upload/slip).
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/upload/image base64-encodes image inline and returns a data URL stored in settings. For party logos / candidate photos this stuffs hundreds of KB into the settings document and into every GET /api/settings response. At scale recommend object storage (S3/local fs) + URL reference. Acceptable for MVP.

GET /api/voters/{id}/slip-data does not filter booth lookup by org_id (line 1158: `db.booths.find_one({'id': v['booth_id']})`). Since voter is already org-scoped this is functionally safe, but defense-in-depth would add org_id to all sub-queries.

DEFAULT_SETTINGS still mutated via `dict(DEFAULT_SETTINGS)` at multiple sites — fine, but if a candidate_* field is added later, ensure migration for existing orgs (currently new fields just appear as None / empty for old orgs).
server.py size (1633 lines) is past the 700-line threshold — strongly recommend modularization before adding more endpoints.
Image upload accepts any image/* content_type from the client; consider also validating magic bytes (PIL or imghdr) to reject text/binary masquerading as image/png. Low priority.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login, AAP isolation, slip page, branding tab all working)

test_credentials: AAP: access_key=AAP-MUM-W20-2026, admin=abhishek@aap.org/abhishek123, agent=priya@aap.org/agent123. VIQSO: access_key=VIQSO-2026, admin=admin@crm.com/admin123, worker=worker@crm.com/worker123. Super-Admin master key: VIQSO-MASTER-2026-XKL9PQR4.
seed_data_creation: Cleared db.login_attempts and removed 5 leftover TEST_ voters in VIQSO org (from prior iterations) so counts match the spec (120 VIQSO / 45 AAP). Iter-3 tests only create one TEST_iter3_bio settings field which is restored at test end. No persistent test data left behind.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run regression with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. If 429 lockout hit: clear with `python -c "from pymongo import MongoClient; import os; from dotenv import load_dotenv; load_dotenv('/app/backend/.env'); MongoClient(os.environ['MONGO_URL'])[os.environ['DB_NAME']].login_attempts.delete_many({})"`. AAP and VIQSO orgs are auto-seeded on app startup (seed_sample_client_org). Iter-3 file /app/backend/tests/test_iter3.py covers AAP login (3 tests), cross-org isolation (3), voter slip + worker scope (2), image upload validation (4), per-org settings PUT isolation (1).
rca of the issue: N/A — no blocking issues found in iter-3. Both iter-2 frontend blockers (access_key field on Login.jsx, SuperAdmin organizations list) have been fixed by main agent and verified by playwright run on this iteration.
